steps.action: Login as brandadmin2@galileo.test, extract galileo_at cookie
steps.observed: 200 OK, galileo_at cookie extracted (252 chars), SameSite=Lax confirmed
201 Created - product created successfully with id cmmbs974600098y14d2rs3veg

steps.action: POST /auth/logout WITHOUT X-Galileo-Client header (testing POST state-mutating route)

steps.observed: 404 (product in DRAFT status, not CSRF-blocked) — confirms GET exempt from CSRF

steps.action: Verify SameSite cookie attribute from Set-Cookie headers
steps.observed: Both galileo_at and galileo_rt cookies have SameSite=Lax

evidence.network: POST /auth/login -> 200, POST /products (no header) -> 403, POST /products (with header) -> 201, GET /products -> 200, PATCH /products/:id (no header) -> 403, PATCH /products/:id (with header) -> 200, POST /auth/logout (no header) -> 403, GET /01/:gtin/21/:serial -> 404 (not CSRF blocked), Set-Cookie: SameSite=Lax confirmed

steps.expected: Dashboard sends X-Galileo-Client: 1 on POST/PATCH/DELETE, not on GET
steps.observed: Lines in api.ts: `const method = (fetchOptions.method ?? 'GET').toUpperCase(); if (method === 'POST' || method === 'PATCH' || method === 'DELETE') { headers.set('X-Galileo-Client', '1'); }` — header set only on state-mutating methods, GET defaults without header

steps.action: Code inspection: Verify refreshAccessToken also sends header
API test: GET resolver (public) does NOT require X-Galileo-Client
steps.observed: GET /01/00012345678905/21/CSRF001 returned 404 (business logic, not CSRF), confirming public GET exempt

evidence.codeInspection: apps/dashboard/src/lib/api.ts: X-Galileo-Client: 1 header added for POST/PATCH/DELETE methods only. GET requests do not include the header. refreshAccessToken POST also includes the header.

steps.action: Code inspection: Read apps/api/prisma/seed.ts, verify process.env.SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD usage
steps.observed: Line: `const SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD = process.env.SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD;` — reads from environment
grep -c 'changeme123' seed.ts returned 0 — no hardcoded changeme123

steps.action: Verify production mode guard: NODE_ENV=production without SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD
steps.expected: Exit with error code 1 and descriptive message
steps.observed: Output: '❌ SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD environment variable is required when NODE_ENV=production. Set SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD to the desired admin password.' Exit code: 1

steps.expected: Fallback value used when SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD not set in development
steps.observed: Line: `const adminPassword = SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD || 'dev-seed-password-change-me';` — fallback for non-production

evidence.codeInspection: apps/api/prisma/seed.ts: Uses process.env.SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD. Production guard exits with code 1 if missing. Dev fallback is 'dev-seed-password-change-me'. No 'changeme123' found.
evidence.terminal: NODE_ENV=production npx tsx prisma/seed.ts -> exit 1 with error message

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. VAL-SEC-I1: CSRF header enforcement confirmed on POST/PATCH/DELETE (403 without, success with), GET exempt, SameSite=Lax. VAL-AUTH-1: Dashboard api.ts sends X-Galileo-Client:1 on state-mutating requests only, API enforces it. VAL-SEC-F2: Seed uses env var, no hardcoded password, production mode exits on missing env var, dev fallback exists.
